claims.evidence_text: Wheat 'UP 262' was sown 23 cm apart in rows on 2 December and 18 November with a seed rate of 100 kglha
claims.claim_summary: Wheat cv. UP 262 sown at a seed rate of 100 kg/ha.

claims.evidence_text: Wheat 'UP 262' was sown 23 cm apart in rows on 2 December and 18 November
claims.claim_summary: Wheat sown in rows spaced 23 cm apart.

claims.evidence_text: application of 1FC irrigation and 100kg Nha, of which 50%top-dressed just after irrigation, showed beneficial effect in optimizing the growth, yield and nitrogen-use efficiency of wheat crop
claims.claim_summary: 100 kg N/ha (50% top-dressed after irrigation) optimized wheat growth, yield and N-use efficiency on alluvial sandy-loam soil; 50 kg N/ha was insufficient.

claims.evidence_text: Nitrogen 100 kglha and half of nitrogen when top-dressed just after irrigation recorded greater grain and straw yields. ... Nitrogen top dressed after irrigation (at CRI stage) showed better result than top dressed before irrigation.
claims.claim_summary: Half of the N top-dressed just after irrigation at crown-root initiation gave higher yields than N applied before irrigation (which enhances leaching loss).

claims.evidence_text: Half dose of N and the full dose of 50 kg P2?, and 50 kg K20/ha were applied basal in the form of urea, single superphosphate and rnuriate of potash respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Full basal dose of 50 kg P2O5/ha applied to wheat.

claims.evidence_text: Half dose of N and the full dose of 50 kg P2?, and 50 kg K20/ha were applied basal in the form of urea, single superphosphate and rnuriate of potash respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Full basal dose of 50 kg K2O/ha applied to wheat.

claims.evidence_text: on sandy-loam soil, having 222.94, 23.63 and 189.20 kgha of available N, P and K, respectively, with organic carbon content 0.53% and pH 6.7.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat field experiment soil was sandy-loam with pH 6.7.
